name: Mooney's Sticky Monster Bones
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine marinade ingredients and marinate the meat in the refrigerator overnight.
2. To freeze, place meat and marinade in a ziploc bag and refrigerate the night before grilling.
3. Set up the grill using hickory chunks of wood and pour beef broth into the drip pan. Add dry rib seasoning.
4. Remove ribs from marinade and drain well. Liberally brush with barbecue seasoning.
5. Place the ribs on the grill over the drip pan and smoke for 2 1/2 to 3 hours.
6. During the final hour, add barbecue sauce at least two to three times.
7. The ribs will hold nicely in a 200-degree oven for several hours if covered.
8. Serve with extra sauce.
Typed by Annette Johnsen
Source: Kansas City Barbecue Society
Submitted By: WCRAFT@IX.NETCOM.COM (William Craft) on 06-22-95; 0609
